title: Fact families
description: Practice relating multiplication and division equations using fact families.
topic_path: Module 3: Multiplication and division with units of 0, 1, 6–9, and multiples of 10
Topic E: Analysis of patterns and problem solving including units of 0 and 1

hints:
Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{56}$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of each group}}$ is $\maroonD8$.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$?

There are $\greenD7$ equal groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{28}$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of each group}}$ is $\maroonD4$.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$?

There are $\greenD7$ equal groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

[[☃ image 1]]

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{45}$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of each group}}$ is $\maroonD9$.




What is the $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$?

There are $\greenD5$ equal groups.



[[☃ image 1]]

Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{24}$.

The $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD3$.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$?

There are $\maroonD8$ in each group.

[[☃ image 1]]

Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD8$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ is $\maroonD9$.

The $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{72}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, 

$\begin{align}
\greenD{8} \times \maroonD{9} = \purpleC{72}\\\\
 \purpleC{72} \div \maroonD9 = \greenD{8} \\\\
 \purpleC{72} =\maroonD9 \times \greenD{8} \\\\
\maroonD9 = \purpleC{72} \div\greenD{8}
\end{align}$

Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

[[☃ image 1]]

In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{24}$.

The $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD4$.



What is the $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$?

There are $\maroonD6$ in each group.


[[☃ image 1]]

Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \times \greenD{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleC{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \greenD{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonD{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleC{\text{total}} \div \maroonD{\text{size of groups}} =\greenD{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD6$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ is $\maroonD7$.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$?

The total is $\purpleC{42}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equations show the same relationship as $72=9\times8$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\tealE{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonE{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleE{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonE{\text{size of groups}} \times \tealE{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleE{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleE{\text{total}} \div \tealE{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonE{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleE{\text{total}} \div \maroonE{\text{size of groups}} =\tealE{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleE{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleE{72}$.

The $\maroonE{\text{size of each group}}$ is $\maroonE8$.

The $\tealE{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\tealE9$.

[[☃ image 1]]

These number sentences show the same relationship:

-  $8 \times 9 = 72$
- $72 \div 9 = 8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equations show the same relationship as $35=5\times7$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Multiplication and division are related:

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{number of groups}} \times \maroonE{\text{size of groups}} = \purpleE{\text{total}} \\\\
\maroonE{\text{size of groups}} \times \blueE{\text{number of groups}} = \purpleE{\text{total}} \\\\
\\\\
\purpleE{\text{total}} \div \blueE{\text{number of groups}} =\maroonE{\text{size of groups}} \\\\
\purpleE{\text{total}} \div \maroonE{\text{size of groups}} =\blueE{\text{number of groups}}
\end{align}$

In this fact family, the $\purpleE{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleE{35}$.

The $\maroonE{\text{size of each group}}$ is $\maroonE5$.

The $\blueE{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\blueE7$.

[[☃ image 1]]

These number sentences show the same relationship:

-  $7 \times 5 = 35$
- $5= 35 \div 7$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which equations can the array represent?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In this fact family, the $\purpleC{\text{total}}$ is $\purpleC{63}$.

The $\greenD{\text{number of the groups}}$ is $\greenD7$.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of each group}}$ is $\maroonD9$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Multiplication: $\greenD{7}\times\maroonD{9}=\purpleC{63}$

Division: $\purpleC{63} \div \greenD{7} = \maroonD{9}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: An array of 63 circles arranged in 7 rows of 9 circles each.
